title: shm_size is ignored when using docker-compose with podman backend
body:
### Issue Description

The `shm_size` parameter is ignored when using docker-compose with a podman backend

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Run this docker-compose.yml file

```
version: '3.3'
services:
    ubuntu:
        image: 'ubuntu:latest'
        shm_size: '2gb'
```

### Describe the results you received

The `shm_size` parameter is ignored and the default `shm` size is used

### Describe the results you expected

`df /dev/shm` should return 2GB of size available

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

almalinux 9.1 LXC on proxmox, rootless container, rootful podman

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: You would not have a simple repeater would you?

> You would not have a simple repeater would you?

Install guide for docker-compose with a podman backend: https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-docker-compose

I'm using this `docker-compose` binary https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/download/v2.17.2/docker-compose-linux-x86_64

```
version: '3.3'
services:
    ubuntu:
	image: 'ubuntu:latest'
        shm_size: '3gb'
        command: sh -c 'df /dev/shm'
```


```
➜  a  docker-compose up
[+] Running 2/0
 ⠿ Network a_default     Created                                           0.0s
 ⠿ Container a-ubuntu-1  Created                                           0.0s
Attaching to a-ubuntu-1
a-ubuntu-1  | Filesystem     1K-blocks  Used Available Use% Mounted on
a-ubuntu-1  | shm              3145728     0   3145728   0% /dev/shm
```

```
version: '3.3'
services:
    ubuntu:
	image: 'ubuntu:latest'
        shm_size: '3000gb'
        command: sh -c 'df /dev/shm
```

```
➜  a docker-compose up
[+] Running 2/0
 ⠿ Network a_default     Created                                           0.0s
 ⠿ Container a-ubuntu-1  Created                                           0.0s
Attaching to a-ubuntu-1
a-ubuntu-1  | Filesystem      1K-blocks  Used  Available Use% Mounted on
a-ubuntu-1  | shm            3145728000     0 3145728000   0% /dev/shm
```


it looks like the gb prefix is ignored, or there's a /1000 scale, as I get 3GB when putting 3000GB as a value
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

```
 $ podman run --shm-size=3000gb alpine mount | grep shm
shm on /dev/shm type tmpfs (rw,context="system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c365,c727",nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,size=3145728000k,uid=3267,gid=3267,inode64)
$ podman run --shm-size=3gb alpine mount | grep shm
shm on /dev/shm type tmpfs (rw,context="system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c52,c321",nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,size=3145728k,uid=3267,gid=3267,inode64)
```

```
# docker run --shm-size=3gb alpine mount | grep shm
shm on /dev/shm type tmpfs (rw,seclabel,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,size=3145728k,inode64)
# docker run --shm-size=3000gb alpine mount | grep shm
shm on /dev/shm type tmpfs (rw,seclabel,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,size=3145728000k,inode64)
```
Docker and Podman work the same way in my testing.
